comment: Shared model spec consumed by BOTH the browser (TF.js builder) and the Python model-builder (Keras builder). Keep this the single source of truth for the 3 selectable architectures so the two sides never disagree. All models: input shape [input_len, 1] -> output shape [input_len, channels_out]. Every layer is a 1D convolution with 'same' padding so the output length equals the input length. The final layer MUST have filters == channels_out.

architectures.name: FIR-1 - single conv, two learned FIR filters
Wide-kernel CNN - large receptive field (k=33)
architectures.comment: Reaches a large receptive field with wide kernels instead of dilation, because TensorFlow.js cannot backprop through dilated convolutions (training happens in the browser).
